content.content:
=== PLAN ===
Build a browser-based Yew/Rust/WASM APL environment for COR24. Single-panel REPL with printer-style emulation, floating hardware panel (S2 switch, D2 LED, UART TX/RX), demo dropdown, file upload, and reset. Phase 1: scaffold Yew project with Trunk, wire cor24-emulator path dep. Phase 2: REPL panel (scrolling output, 6-space indent prompt, keyboard capture) + control bar. Phase 3: emulator integration (EmulatorBridge, UART I/O bridge, batch execution, pre-assemble APL interpreter via build.rs). Phase 4: hardware panel + demo registry + file upload. Phase 5: polish, responsive design, GitHub Pages deployment. Phase 6: APL glyph prettification display modes.

=== STEPS ===
  [x] 001-scaffold-yew-project [production]: Scaffold the Yew/Rust/WASM project for web-sw-cor24-apl. Follow the exact pat...
  [ ] 002-repl-panel [legacy]: Create the REPL panel component. Follow the patterns from web-sw-cor24-pcode'... <-- YOU ARE HERE

=== CURRENT STEP: 002-repl-panel [legacy] ===
Status: pending
Description: Create the REPL panel component. Follow the patterns from web-sw-cor24-pcode'...

=== PROMPT ===
Create the REPL panel component. Follow the patterns from web-sw-cor24-pcode's debugger component.

1. Create src/repl.rs with ReplPanel function_component:
   - Scrolling output area (div with overflow-y: auto, ref for auto-scroll)
   - Output buffer as Vec<String> rendered as lines
   - Input line at bottom with 6-space indent prompt
   - Keyboard capture (onkeydown on the container div) handling:
     - Enter: take current input, add prompt+input to output, echo input back (no emulator yet), clear input
     - Backspace: remove last char from input buffer
     - Printable chars: append to input buffer
   - Auto-scroll to bottom on new output
   - Printer-style behavior: new output appends at bottom

2. Update src/lib.rs:
   - Add mod repl
   - Replace placeholder with <ReplPanel />

3. Create/update src/app.css with REPL panel styles:
   - .repl-panel: flex container, full height
   - .repl-output: scrolling area, Catppuccin base background, monospace text
   - .repl-input-line: fixed at bottom, 6-space indent, blue prompt color
   - .repl-cursor: blinking cursor indicator
   - Use Catppuccin colors: base bg, text fg, blue for prompt, green for output

4. Verify trunk build compiles and the REPL panel renders with echo-back behavior.

=== WHEN DONE ===
Run: agentrail begin    (if not yet started)
Run: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --next-slug <slug> --next-prompt "next instructions"
  Or: agentrail complete --summary "what you did" --done   (if this is the last step)

file.content:
[package]
name = "web-sw-cor24-apl"
version = "0.1.0"
edition = "2024"
authors = ["Michael A Wright"]
license = "MIT"
description = "Browser-based APL environment on COR24"

[lib]
crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]

[dependencies]
cor24-emulator = { path = "../sw-cor24-emulator", default-features = false }
cor24-isa = { path = "../sw-cor24-emulator/isa", features = ["serde"] }
yew = { version = "0.21", features = ["csr"] }
wasm-bindgen = "0.2"
web-sys = { version = "0.3", features = ["console", "HtmlInputElement", "HtmlDivElement", "KeyboardEvent", "Element", "Window", "Document"] }
gloo = "0.11"
console_error_panic_hook = "0.1"

[build-dependencies]
cor24-emulator = { path = "../sw-cor24-emulator", default-features = false }

[profile.release]
opt-level = "z"
lto = true

1	//! P-code VM debugger component — two-level debugging with p-code semantic
2	//! layer (primary) and COR24 host implementation layer (secondary drill-down).
3	
4	use crate::config;
5	use crate::demos::DEMOS;
6	use cor24_emulator::{EmulatorCore, StopReason};
7	use gloo::timers::callback::Timeout;
8	use std::collections::{HashMap, VecDeque};
9	use web_sys::HtmlInputElement;
10	use yew::prelude::*;
11	
12	/// Execution batch size per tick (instructions).
13	const BATCH_SIZE: u64 = 50_000;
14	
15	/// Tick interval in milliseconds.
16	const TICK_MS: u32 = 25;
17	
18	/// Number of COR24 disassembly lines to show before current PC.
19	const DISASM_BEFORE: usize = 8;
20	
21	/// Number of COR24 disassembly lines to show after current PC.
22	const DISASM_AFTER: usize = 16;
23	
24	/// P-code opcode names indexed by opcode number.
25	const PCODE_NAMES: &[(u8, &str)] = &[
26	    (0x00, "halt"),
27	    (0x01, "push"),
28	    (0x02, "push_s"),
29	    (0x03, "dup"),
30	    (0x04, "drop"),
31	    (0x05, "swap"),
32	    (0x06, "over"),
33	    (0x10, "add"),
34	    (0x11, "sub"),
35	    (0x12, "mul"),
36	    (0x13, "div"),
37	    (0x14, "mod"),
38	    (0x15, "neg"),
39	    (0x16, "and"),
40	    (0x17, "or"),
41	    (0x18, "xor"),
42	    (0x19, "not"),
43	    (0x1A, "shl"),
44	    (0x1B, "shr"),
45	    (0x20, "eq"),
46	    (0x21, "ne"),
47	    (0x22, "lt"),
48	    (0x23, "le"),
49	    (0x24, "gt"),
50	    (0x25, "ge"),
51	    (0x30, "jmp"),
52	    (0x31, "jz"),
53	    (0x32, "jnz"),
54	    (0x33, "call"),
55	    (0x34, "ret"),
56	    (0x35, "calln"),
57	    (0x36, "trap"),
58	    (0x40, "enter"),
59	    (0x41, "leave"),
60	    (0x42, "loadl"),
61	    (0x43, "storel"),
62	    (0x44, "loadg"),
63	    (0x45, "storeg"),
64	    (0x46, "addrl"),
65	    (0x47, "addrg"),
66	    (0x48, "loada"),
67	    (0x49, "storea"),
68	    (0x4A, "loadn"),
69	    (0x4B, "storen"),
70	    (0x50, "load"),
71	    (0x51, "store"),
72	    (0x52, "loadb"),
73	    (0x53, "storeb"),
74	    (0x60, "sys"),
75	];
76	
77	/// vm_state struct field offsets (each field is a 24-bit word = 3 bytes).
78	mod vm_offsets {
79	    pub const PC: u32 = 0;
80	    pub const ESP: u32 = 3;
81	    pub const CSP: u32 = 6;
82	    pub const FP_VM: u32 = 9;
83	    pub const GP: u32 = 12;
84	    pub const HP: u32 = 15;
85	    pub const CODE: u32 = 18;
86	    pub const STATUS: u32 = 21;
87	    pub const TRAP_CODE: u32 = 24;
88	}
89	
90	/// Decoded p-code instruction.
91	struct PcodeInstr {
92	    addr: u32,
93	    name: &'static str,
94	    operand: Option<u32>,
95	    size: u32,
96	}
97	
98	/// A decoded call frame from the call stack.
99	struct CallFrame {
100	    /// Return p-code PC.

content.text:
# /mw-cp — Mike Wright's Checkpoint Process

You are executing a disciplined checkpoint commit. Follow every phase in order.
Do NOT skip any phase, even if it seems clean. Report status after each phase.

---

## Phase 1 — Pre-Commit Checks

Run all of the following that apply to this project. Stop and report failures before proceeding.

1. **Format**: `cargo fmt --check` (or `rustfmt --check` on specific files)
2. **Lint**: `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`
3. **Build**: `cargo build` (debug is fine; surface any compile errors)
4. **Tests**: `cargo test` — all tests must pass
5. **Unused deps** (if `cargo-machete` present): `cargo machete`
6. **Project-local checks**: run `just check` or `just pre-commit` if a justfile exists

If any check fails, STOP. Report what failed and ask the user how to proceed.

---

## Phase 2 — Documentation Updates

Review recent changes and update the following as appropriate:

1. **CHANGELOG.md / CHANGES.md** — add an entry under `[Unreleased]` or today's date
2. **STATUS.md** — update current state, what changed, what's in progress
3. **LEARNINGS.md** — capture anything notable: decisions made, gotchas, tradeoffs
4. **Inline docs** — ensure any new public API items have doc comments (`///`)
5. **README.md** — update if any user-facing behavior, config, or setup changed

If none of these files exist, note that and skip gracefully.

---

## Phase 3 — Staged Diff Review

Run `git diff --staged` (or `git diff HEAD` if nothing staged yet).

Summarize what is changing:
- Files modified, added, deleted
- High-level functional description of the change

Then stage all changes: `git add -A`

---

## Phase 4 — Commit

Compose a detailed commit message following Conventional Commits style:
```
<type>(<scope>): <short imperative summary>

<body — explain WHAT changed and WHY, not HOW>
<mention any tradeoffs, alternatives considered, or follow-ups needed>

Refs: <issue/ticket numbers if applicable>
Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
```

Types: `feat`, `fix`, `refactor`, `docs`, `test`, `chore`, `perf`, `build`

If  was provided, incorporate it as context for the message.

Execute: `git commit -m "<message>"`

---

## Phase 5 — Push

1. Check current branch: `git branch --show-current`
2. Check remote: `git remote -v`
3. Push: `git push origin <branch>`

If the push is rejected (non-fast-forward), STOP and report — do NOT force push without explicit user confirmation.

---

## Phase 6 — Checkpoint Summary

Report a brief summary:
- ✅ Checks passed
- 📝 Docs updated (list files)
- 🔖 Commit hash and message
- 🚀 Pushed to remote/branch

Flag any items that were skipped or need follow-up.
```

---

## Usage
```
/mw-cp
/mw-cp adds OAuth token refresh with retry logic

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(repl): add REPL panel component with echo-back behavior

Implement the ReplPanel component following the P-code debugger's
component patterns. Features:
- Scrolling output area with auto-scroll to bottom
- Keyboard capture (Enter, Backspace, printable chars)
- 6-space indent prompt matching printer-style emulation
- Echo-back of input (emulator integration comes later)
- Blinking block cursor via timer-based toggle
- Catppuccin Mocha themed: green output, blue prompt, base background

Replaces the placeholder content in the main app with the live REPL.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
